# backend/middleware/idempotency.py
# auto-generated explainer · 2026-06-11T10:01:44Z

## Purpose
Idempotency-Key middleware · Iter 25 · C2 closure.

Per §47.7 (write paths · safe retry contract):
  · POST/PUT/PATCH endpoints honor `Idempotency-Key` header
  · First request: process + cache response (status + body) by key
  · Retries with same key + same body hash: return cached response · skip handler
  · 24-hour TTL (in-memory · production would use Redis)

Per §57.7 honest: cache survives ONLY for the process lifetime
(in-memory dict). Cross-restart durability needs Redis.

## Imports
- from `__future__` import annotations
- from `_` import hashlib
- from `_` import time
- from `typing` import Any
- from `starlette.middleware.base` import BaseHTTPMiddleware
- from `starlette.requests` import Request
- from `starlette.responses` import Response, JSONResponse

(no CLI / env inputs — library module or implicit)

## Process
### Classes
- `IdempotencyMiddleware`: Honor Idempotency-Key for write methods · cache successful responses.
### Functions
- `_body_hash(body)`: (no docstring)
- `_evict_expired()`: (no docstring)

## Outputs
- Return values from functions above

## Notable inline comments
- Iter 26 · explicit Response.headers set on cache hit per Starlette doc
- Rebuild request stream so handler can read body
- Cache only successful responses
